content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tree consisting of NNN nodes, rooted at node 111. The parent of the ith‚ÄÖ‚Ää(2‚â§i‚â§N)i^{th}\;(2\le i \le N)ith(2‚â§i‚â§N) node in the tree is the node PiP_iPi‚Äã.

Chef wants to assign a binary value (`0` or `1`) to every node of the tree. The **Xor-value** of a node is defined as the bitwise XOR of all the binary values present in the subtree of that node.

Help Chef to assign values to the nodes of the tree in such a way that the sum of Xor-value of all the NNN nodes in the tree is **exactly** KKK.

It can be shown that such an assignment always exists. If there are multiple possible answers, you may print **any of them**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK, denoting the number of vertices in the tree and the required sum of Xor-value respectively.
The second line of each test case contains N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 space-separated integers P2,P3,‚Ä¶,PNP_2, P_3,\ldots, P_NP2‚Äã,P3‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã, where PiP_iPi‚Äã denotes the parent-node of the ithi^{th}ith node.


Output Format
For each test case, print on a new line a binary string of length NNN where the ith‚ÄÖ‚Ää(1‚â§i‚â§N)i^{th}\;(1\le i \le N)ith(1‚â§i‚â§N) character of the string denotes the binary value assigned to node iii. If there are multiple ways to assign values to the node, you can do it in any way.
constraints: 1‚â§Pi<i1 \leq P_i \lt i1‚â§Pi‚Äã<i for each 2‚â§i‚â§N2\le i \le N2‚â§i‚â§N
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The Xor-value of both nodes is 0.
Test case 222: The Xor-value of the node 111 is 111  because the subtree of the node 111 contains the values 1,0,11, 0, 11,0,1 and 1‚äï0‚äï0=11\oplus 0 \oplus 0 = 11‚äï0‚äï0=1. The nodes 2,32, 32,3 have Xor-value 000 because the subtree of both nodes contains the value 000.
Test case 333: The Xor-value of the nodes 1,41, 41,4 is 111 and the remaining nodes have Xor-value 000.
